## Stewards and Working Group Coordination Leads (WGCL)

Stewards and WGCL may be active contributors in more than one WG.  Stewards may also be WGCL although not always. WGCL may also be Stewards although not always.

* Stewards:
  * are nominated by another Steward,
  * are the MVV super-spreaders,
  * carry the know-how of the TEC (e.g., how we are organized, whom to ask about what, what projects are active across multiple WGs),
  * represent the TEC to the larger community,
  * raise, debate, resolve, coordinate, advocate, propagate,
  * are the civil servants of the community.
  * Expected time commitment: At least 10+ hours per week to keep active and “on top” of what’s happening, attend the Weekly, Council and Community calls, other WG calls, etc.
* Working Group Coordination Leads (WGCL):
  * are appointed by the Steward or WG,
  * are responsible for streams of work contained within a WG,
  * join Scrum events for the coordination and surfacing inter-wg dependencies.
  * Expected time commitment: At least 10+ hours per week. Attending the Sprint planning & retro, Weekly call, Council call, WG calls, etc.

## Funding

This 3-month funding proposal is on the Forum for Advice Process and will be submitted following the launch of the Commons upgrade.
[https://forum.tecommons.org/t/steward-wg-3-month-funding-proposal/713](https://forum.tecommons.org/t/steward-wg-3-month-funding-proposal/713)

## Terms

* The Stewardship term is 6 months.
* Multiple terms are possible.
* Stewards WG funding will provide a nominal compensation in show of appreciation of the value Stewards bring to the community.
* We will have two “statement of commitment” windows per year to allow Stewards to either share their commitment to continuing to steward in the TEC or fall out gracefully from the role if they are no longer active. (Compensation may have stopped before this point in cases where Stewards are no longer able to remain active and present).
* At that time, Stewards who wish to continue may post a statement of commitment in the Forum post created for that purpose. If a Steward chooses not to continue as a Steward due to personal or professional obligations, they can either request to be offboarded or simply not post a statement during the confirmation window. In either case, the Steward will be offboarded following the self-nominated offboarding process later in this document.
* This process will start on Feb 1-15, 2022. The following period will be Aug 1-15.

## Commitments

The three key traits that describe Stewards in our Community are:

1. **active and present** - joins calls and participate in community engagement across one or more working groups.
2. **servant leaders** - the goal is to serve by valuing others, being humble and putting others first, listening receptively and nonjudgmentally, give trust to others and caring for the community.
3. **in the know** -  know what is happening in a number of different Working Groups, spread our Mission, Vision and Values, guide community members to find what they are looking for.

TEC Stewards are, first and foremost, committed to advancing token engineering! We are **present, active and engaged** in one or more TEC Working Group. We represent the TEC ethos within the community. We are the [MVV](https://app.gitbook.com/o/-MRe_DImEWCFDsZ_TQd2/s/-MRe_M_mUIIB11A-MQyZ/what-is-the-tec/mission-vision-and-values) super spreaders.

And, we are committed to follow cultural processes based on Ostrom’s principles:

1. Clearly defined boundaries
2. Congruence between appropriation and provision
3. Collective choice arrangements
4. Monitoring
5. Graduated sanctions
6. Conflict resolution mechanisms
7. Minimal recognition of rights to organize
8. Nested enterprises

We consider ourselves the civil servants of the TEC. Servant leadership is a practice we continue to build and develop individually and as a group. The 10 keys servant leadership traits are:

1. listening
2. empathy
3. stewardship
4. foresight
5. persuasion
6. conceptualization
7. awareness
8. healing
9. commitment to the growth and development of people
10. building community

## Goals

* ### What goals would you like to achieve with this working group?

  The main goals of the Community Stewards are:

* **Shared vision**. To ensure all Stewards are aware of the other WG priorities and activities and are focused on our shared roadmap.
* **Effective coordination.** To plan and coordinate interdependent activities across multiple WG.
* **Maintain knowledge.** To maintain an active group of community stewards who are well versed in how the TEC functions and are ready to pitch in to help advance TEC goals.
* **Vision and Values.** Maintain alignment with the TEC mission vision and values.
* **Steward support.** To allow stewards to gather and discuss what we are feeling in the community and any issues or blockers they may be experiencing themselves or within their WG. This weekly space allows raised problems to be solved in a group setting with the benefit of collective intelligence.
* **Nurture Community.** Nurturing the overall culture/growth of the TEC community

* ### How will this working group benefit the TEC community according to its near term mission?

* Coordination between WGs will facilitate achieving the goals of the TEC through timely addressing issues and potential issues, shared knowledge and experience, and greater alignment.
* The various working group leads can sync up and surface issues and strategize all together to keep the TEC moving forward.
* It will help us move in the same direction towards a healthy and pro-active culture
* It will keep the horizontal structure of the TEC evolving to support TE projects and community

* ### What would be considered a success vs. failure?

  Success:
* A constant and strong coordination between all of the working groups.
* Visibility on WG outputs and interdependencies.
* A nurtured community that can grow while maintaining healthy cultural practices.
* Allowing for and protecting organic growth and decentralized activities/initiatives.
* Community engagement & high vibes!
* Every member of the community can request assistance when they are blocked and/ or request general feedback.
* Fractal development of initiatives.
* Have a clear process for onboarding Stewards.
* Have a clear process to offboard Stewards.

	Failure:

* WG developing their objectives without connection between each other.
* Taking over “control” / too much centralization for decision making.
* Not staying aligned with the vision/mission.
* Not supporting the community when needed.
* Lack of transparency and communication.

* ### Please break down the goals into a roadmap with clear milestones

Zenhub [TEC Coordination](https://app.zenhub.com/workspaces/tec-coordination-workspace-5fad0d3fbbe4da0011c2f40d/board?repos=306706322) reflects all the work in our Product Backlog and all the work currently in progress in each Sprint.

* ### What is the deadline?

This WG will continue for as long as it continues to benefit the TEC. This is a living document that should be updated periodically.

* ### Working style & pace

Public discussions happen on the public #stewards channel on TEC Discord.

Private Stewards conversations happen on a private #stewards Telegram channel. The reasoning behind having a private channel is to [REDACTED] greater intimate discussion amongst the Stewards and to have greater team cohesion.

Meetings:

* Sprint Planning: Every other Fri.
* Sprint Retrospective: Every other Thurs.
* Stewards Council: Every other Thurs.
* Stewards Weekly: Every Tues.

We practice the light framework of Scrum.

Scrum Theory
Scrum is founded on empiricism and lean thinking. Empiricism asserts that knowledge comes from experience and making decisions based on what is observed. Lean thinking reduces waste and focuses on the essentials.

Scrum Practice
We follow a 2-week sprint cadence. We have bi-weekly Sprint Planning and Retrospective sessions and a weekly Community call that serves the purpose of Sprint Review.

* Sprint Planning. The purpose is to :
  * Refine the product backlog.
  * Prioritize issues against what else there is to be done.
  * Plan the next Sprint worth of work to be completed.
* Sprint Review. The purpose is to :
  * Inspect progress toward the Sprint Goal.
  * Show progress to the community.
* Sprint Retrospective. The purpose is to :
  * Plans ways to increase quality and effectiveness. How the last sprint went w.r.t. individuals, interactions, processes, tools and definition of done.
  * Identify the most helpful changes to improve its effectiveness.
  * The most impactful are addressed as soon as possible, possibly even coming into the new sprint.

## Onboarding & Offboarding

![][image1]

The purpose of having a process for onboarding and offboarding is to define clear boundaries around stewardship roles in the TEC.

#### Onboarding

* A Steward is nominated by another Steward and requires a second confirmation.
* There is a period of 1 week where any other Steward can block the nomination.
* Nomination blocks will be discussed within the Stewards WG and if it is not resolvable, then a Gravity form will be filed for mediation.
* One or more Stewards will invite the nominee to become a Steward.
  * If they cannot accept, then nothing changes.
  * If they accept, we host an onboarding ceremony and run the checklist of onboarding a new Steward. (Warm welcome from all the Stewards, invitation to the weekly call and public and private #stewards channel, explanation of expectations, Scrum and Zenhub board, channels.)

####

##### Onboarding process

* Onboarding process
  * Onboarding checklist
    * Manifesto: add to the [Steward manifesto](#).
    * Ancillary docs: add Steward to Stewards list
    * Discord: Steward role assigned
    * Telegram: add to the TEC Stewards group
    * Github: add to [Stewards team](https://github.com/orgs/CommonsBuild/teams/community-stewards/members)
  * Onboarding ceremony
    * Generally held during one of the Stewards’ weekly calls
    * Opening question: “What did you think the first time you met x?”
    * Pass around those in the call

#### Offboarding

Onboarding is not necessarily a permanent action. Former Stewards are welcome to return when their personal and professional obligations permit.

Possible reasons:

* Self-offboarding for any personal or professional reasons
* Breaking our code of conduct.
* No longer “active and present”. (ex. uncommunicated absence for 1 month or not participating in the transversal coordination sessions if that you are a WGCL for your WG).
* No longer “in the know”. This is subjective but it includes not really knowing what is happening in the TEC or repeatedly sharing incorrect information within the community.

##### Offboarding process

* Self-offboarding
  * A steward can inform the whole WG or any of the Stewards in the WG. This will kick off the steps of the Offboarding process.
* Nominated offboarding
  * A Steward can nominate another Steward for offboarding and provide the reasoning. This can happen on the private Stewards TG group or on the weekly call.
  * There is a period of 1 week where any other Steward can block the offboarding.
  * If no blockers, the reasons for the off boarding are communicated to the Steward and the Offboarding process starts.
  * If there is a blocker, it is discussed in our weekly sync and if not resolved then it is routed to Gravity for mediation.
* Offboarding process
  * Offboarding checklist
    * Manifesto: remove from the [Steward manifesto](#).
    * Ancillary docs: remove offboarded Steward from Stewards list
    * Discord: Steward role is unassigned.
    * Telegram: remove from the TEC Stewards group
    * Github: remove from [Stewards team](https://github.com/orgs/CommonsBuild/teams/community-stewards/members)
  * Offboarding ceremony
    * Pick and share their top contributions to the TEC and to the Cultural build.
    * Open floor and pass
    * Open floor to offboarded Steward

###
